rows.aiFlashcards: AI flashcards from PDFs and docs

q1.question: How many cards can I make for free?
q1.answer: 100 cards per month on the free plan. No account required for one-off conversions — drop a file and download a deck.

q2.question: Is there a one-time payment option?
q2.answer: Day Pass ({{dayPrice}}) gives 24 hours of unlimited access. Week Pass ({{weekPrice}}) gives 7 days. Both are one-time payments, no subscription required.

q3.question: What is the Unlimited plan?
q3.answer: Unlimited removes the 100-card limit, adds PDF support, lets you run multiple conversions at once, and includes unlimited Anki to Notion imports. Pricing is on the cards above.

q4.question: What about Lifetime?
q4.answer: Lifetime starts at $345, paid once. It includes all Unlimited features plus Auto Sync. Apply on this page; access is granted by review.

q5.question: Can I cancel anytime?
q5.answer: Cancel in one click from your account — no need to email support. On a yearly plan, cancelling stops the next renewal and your access stays through the year you already paid for; unused time is not refunded. Passes are one-time, so there is nothing to cancel.

q6.question: Is there an app?
q6.answer: Yes — 2anki is free on the App Store for iPhone, iPad, and Mac, alongside the web app. Files are parsed on-device. The same plans apply: Day Pass, Week Pass, and Unlimited are available as in-app purchases.

q7.question: What happens to my decks if I stop paying?
q7.answer: They stay yours. Every deck exports as a native .apkg that works in any Anki client, offline, with no lock-in.
